teachingPoint: T99 SeatReservationHandler MUST atomically reserve a seat (TTL hold) BEFORE initiating payment capture. Natural order pay-then-seat creates a double-booking race: two users pay simultaneously for the last seat, both payments succeed, both seat claims succeed, capacity becomes -1. TTL hold ensures exactly-once seat reservation before money moves. SILENT_FAILURE: code passes CI, race condition only visible under concurrent last-ticket production load.
positiveExample: T99 calls reserveSeat(TTL hold) first, confirming atomic hold before calling enqueuePayment(). If payment fails, TTL hold expires and seat returns to available pool.
negativeExample: T99 calls enqueuePayment() first, then claims seat after payment succeeds. Two concurrent users both pay for last seat, both succeed, capacity becomes -1.
discriminatingConstraint: Line index of reserveSeat() call in T99 must be less than line index of enqueuePayment() call. Any code path initiating payment before seat hold is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-1.
keywords: seat-before-payment TTL double-booking reserveSeat enqueuePayment race condition last-ticket

teachingPoint: T113 FraudDetectorHandler MUST fail open when fraud service (F283) is unavailable. Three conditions required simultaneously: try/catch around F283 call, allow-on-catch (return { passed: true }), emit FraudCheckFailed(service_unavailable) audit event. This reverses the default fail=stop pattern established in 8 prior flows. SILENT_FAILURE: missing any one of the three conditions. Missing try/catch crashes T99. Missing allow-on-catch blocks all purchases during outage. Missing audit event makes service_unavailable indistinguishable from fraud detection.
positiveExample: T113: try { result = await F283.check(purchase) } catch (e) { emit FraudCheckFailed({ reason: 'service_unavailable', purchaseId }); return { passed: true } }
negativeExample: T113 catch block returns { passed: false } — blocks all purchases when fraud service is down. No audit event — downtime indistinguishable from fraud.
discriminatingConstraint: T113 catch block must: (1) emit FraudCheckFailed({ reason: service_unavailable }), (2) return { passed: true }. Any catch path returning false is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-2.
keywords: fail-open FraudCheckFailed service_unavailable allow-on-catch fraud detector inline

teachingPoint: T108 GroupBookingCoordinatorHandler MUST wrap ALL group ticket issuances in a single database transaction. A loop outside a transaction is the naively correct pattern — it compiles, unit tests pass. Partial failure only surfaces when one member fails mid-loop in production: members 1-3 have tickets, 4-6 do not. No error thrown. Partial group booking silently created. SILENT_FAILURE class: compiles; partial success only visible under production mid-loop failure.
positiveExample: T108 uses db.transaction(() => groupMembers.map(m => issueTicket(m))). Any member failure rolls back all issuances — all-or-nothing guarantee.
negativeExample: T108 loops through group members with for...of outside any transaction. Member 4 of 6 fails. Members 1-3 have confirmed tickets, 4-6 do not. No error surfaced.
discriminatingConstraint: All ticket issuances for a group are wrapped in ONE db.transaction() call. Any loop outside a transaction is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-4.
keywords: all-or-nothing group booking db.transaction rollback partial success group ticket coordinator

teachingPoint: T105 ComplianceEscalationHandler after max retries MUST simultaneously emit RefundFailed AND push to F284 (manual review queue). A single event has no delivery guarantee — the event may go unhandled, refund disappears, compliance officer never notified. F284 guarantees human review. Missing either action = SILENT_FAILURE. PER_ATTEMPT_IDEMPOTENCY: idempotency key includes attempt counter N, allowing each retry to proceed independently while deduplicating duplicate deliveries of the same attempt.
positiveExample: On exhaustion: emit RefundFailed({ purchaseId, reason: 'MAX_RETRIES_EXCEEDED' }) AND await F284.push(complianceRecord). Both complete before handler returns.
negativeExample: On exhaustion: emit RefundFailed only. F284 push absent. Compliance officer never sees the failed refund. Refund disappears from system.
discriminatingConstraint: Exhaustion path has both RefundFailed emit AND F284.push(). Missing F284 push is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-3.
keywords: compliance escalation exhaustion RefundFailed F284 simultaneous both-required idempotency attempt counter

teachingPoint: T112 FeeCalculatorHandler is INLINE_PURE — called inline by T110, returns FeeBreakdown only. T112 MUST NOT call storeDocument or emit events. If T112 stores fee records independently, a T110 crash after the inline call leaves an orphaned fee record with no corresponding revenue record. Fee and revenue records diverge. The inline-pure execution model keeps fee computation as a synchronous sub-calculation owned entirely by the caller. SILENT_FAILURE: adding storeDocument() to T112 compiles; divergence only visible on caller failure.
positiveExample: T112 is @Injectable() only. Returns FeeBreakdown: { baseFee, platformFee, totalFee }. No storeDocument, no enqueue, no @EventPattern.
negativeExample: T112 calls storeDocument('fee-records', { feeBreakdown, purchaseId }). T110 crashes after T112 returns. Orphaned fee record exists with no revenue record.
discriminatingConstraint: T112 has no storeDocument call and no enqueue call. Any write to database in T112 is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-5.
keywords: inline-pure FeeBreakdown pure computation no storeDocument fee calculator orphaned records

teachingPoint: F275 QR token generation (T102) and validation (T103) is PLATFORM-ONLY. No tenant factory swap permitted. QR token integrity is a security primitive — a tenant swapping F275 could generate tokens that bypass validation, enabling ticket fraud. Contrast with F281 (payout): INJECTABLE for business reasons. F275 and F283 (fraud) are PLATFORM-ONLY for security reasons. The distinction between INJECTABLE and PLATFORM-ONLY is architecture, not preference.
positiveExample: T102 factory dependency declares F275 as PLATFORM-ONLY with no IExternalServiceFactory.createAsync() path for tenant override.
negativeExample: T102 uses IExternalServiceFactory.createAsync('qr-service') — tenants can provide their own QR implementation, creating forged token risk.
discriminatingConstraint: F275 is PLATFORM-ONLY in both T102 and T103 contracts. Any tenant-configurable QR factory declaration is a BUILD_FAILURE per CF-09-6.
keywords: platform-only F275 QR token-forgery trust boundary INJECTABLE vs PLATFORM-ONLY security

teachingPoint: T118 RegistrationAnalyticsHandler is OBSERVABILITY archetype — the entire handler body runs inside a try/catch. Errors are swallowed with a log entry, and the handler returns DataProcessResult.success() regardless. This is the observability contract: analytics must never block the purchase path. T118 errors are silent failures by design — failing analytics recording must not prevent ticket confirmation.
positiveExample: T118 wraps all logic in try { ... analytics work ... return DataProcessResult.success(summary) } catch (e) { log(e); return DataProcessResult.success({ status: 'ANALYTICS_SKIPPED' }) }
negativeExample: T118 returns DataProcessResult.failure('ANALYTICS_ERROR') when recording fails. Purchase path receives failure, ticket confirmation blocked by analytics.
discriminatingConstraint: T118 always returns DataProcessResult.success(). Any code path that returns failure from T118 is a BUILD_FAILURE per OBSERVABILITY archetype contract.
keywords: observability analytics OBSERVABILITY archetype try-catch swallow error never blocks purchase path
seat reserve before payment TTL hold atomic double-booking race condition last-ticket
teachingPoint: Seat reservation MUST precede payment capture. Natural order (pay then claim seat) creates a silent double-booking race under concurrent last-ticket load. TTL hold ensures exactly-once seat claim before money moves — if payment fails, TTL expiry returns seat to pool automatically.
ironRules: reserveSeat(TTL) via F274 MUST appear before enqueuePayment() in T99 handler
Seat hold must be confirmed atomic success before payment is initiated
TTL hold automatically returns seat to available pool on payment failure
No path in T99 may initiate payment without a prior confirmed seat hold

keywords: fail-open fraud service unavailable audit event allow-on-catch three conditions simultaneously
teachingPoint: Security-adjacent inline services (fraud detection) MUST fail open — service unavailability must not block the purchase path. Three conditions required simultaneously: try/catch, allow-on-catch (return { passed: true }), and audit emit (FraudCheckFailed). Missing any one = silent failure class: blocking all purchases OR hiding outages.
ironRules: try/catch wraps the fraud service (F283) call
catch block MUST return { passed: true } — fail open, not fail closed
catch block MUST emit FraudCheckFailed({ reason: 'service_unavailable', purchaseId })
All three conditions required simultaneously — missing any one is BUILD_FAILURE

keywords: group booking db.transaction all-or-nothing rollback partial success loop outside transaction silent failure
teachingPoint: Any loop over a collection of write operations that must all succeed or all fail must be wrapped in a single db.transaction(). A for...of loop outside a transaction compiles, passes all unit tests, but creates partial success on mid-loop production failure: N of M members get tickets, rest do not, no error thrown.
ironRules: All group ticket issuances wrapped in ONE db.transaction() call
Any single member issuance failure triggers full rollback for all members
for...of loop outside transaction is a BUILD_FAILURE — silent partial success
Transaction boundary must encompass ALL write operations in the atomic unit

keywords: inline-pure pure computation no storeDocument no enqueue caller owns writes FeeCalculator orphaned
teachingPoint: Inline-pure services perform computation only — they MUST NOT call storeDocument or enqueue. Adding storage to an inline-pure breaks the caller's ownership contract: if the caller fails after the inline call, the inline service has created an orphaned record with no corresponding parent. The caller owns all writes.
ironRules: T112 must NOT call storeDocument or enqueue
T112 returns FeeBreakdown: { baseFee, platformFee, totalFee } — computation result only
Any database write in an inline-pure = BUILD_FAILURE — orphaned record risk
